item.title: Hello world!
item.dc:creator: newworldfoolproductions
item.content:encoded: Welcome to WordPress. This is your first post. Edit or delete it, then start writing!

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: Hi, this is a comment. To get started with moderating, editing, and deleting comments, please visit the Comments screen in the dashboard. Commenter avatars come from Gravatar.

item.title: Sample Page
item.dc:creator: newworldfoolproductions
item.content:encoded:
This is an example page. It's different from a blog post because it will stay in one place and will show up in your site navigation (in most themes). Most people start with an About page that introduces them to potential site visitors. It might say something like this:

Hi there! I'm a bike messenger by day, aspiring actor by night, and this is my website. I live in Los Angeles, have a great dog named Jack, and I like piña coladas. (And gettin' caught in the rain.)

...or something like this:

The XYZ Doohickey Company was founded in 1971, and has been providing quality doohickeys to the public ever since. Located in Gotham City, XYZ employs over 2,000 people and does all kinds of awesome things for the Gotham community.

As a new WordPress user, you should go to your dashboard to delete this page and create new pages for your content. Have fun!

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: newworldfoolproductions
item.content:encoded:
Who we are

Suggested text: Our website address is: https://newworldfoolproductions.com.

Comments

Suggested text: When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymized string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

Media

Suggested text: If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

Cookies

Suggested text: If you leave a comment on our site you may opt-in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.

If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select "Remember Me", your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

Embedded content from other websites

Suggested text: Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.

Who we share your data with

Suggested text: If you request a password reset, your IP address will be included in the reset email.

How long we retain your data

Suggested text: If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognize and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.

What rights you have over your data

Suggested text: If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.

Where your data is sent

Suggested text: Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.

item.title: The Hunter
item.dc:creator: newworldfoolproductions
item.content:encoded:
Directors: Dylan Gaines

Writers: Dylan Gaines

Producers: Dylan Gaines, George Wang, William O'Bryan

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: An arrogant hunter heads into the woods for what he thinks is a typical day of sport. But things change when it turns out he is the one being hunted…

item.title: Mukbang'd
item.dc:creator: newworldfoolproductions
item.content:encoded:
Director: Dylan Gaines

Writer: Dylan Gaines

Producers: Dylan Gaines, Craig Helfer, William O'Bryan

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: A mukbang influencer begins to film another day of her scarfing and spitting out piles of food. Until a mysterious accident causes things to spiral out of control…

item.title: Unforeseen
item.dc:creator: newworldfoolproductions
item.content:encoded:
Directors: Craig Helfer, Dylan Gaines

Writers: Craig Helfer, Dylan Gaines

Producers: Craig Helfer, Dylan Gaines

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: An ambitious film crew shoots a horror film in what they think is an abandoned warehouse. But as the crew splinters, an unforeseen entity comes to visit

item.title: About
item.dc:creator: newworldfoolproductions
item.content:encoded:
We live in a world drenched in arrogance, greed, and corruption. By invoking the medieval iconography of the “court jester”, New World Fool Productions seeks to illuminate these blatant hypocrisies which society covers up through across a variety of visual media formats.

Dylan Gaines

Dylan Gaines is a writer, director, and producer based in New York City, crafting films that blur the lines between black comedy and horror. Originally from Boston, he received his degree in Cinema and Media Arts from Vanderbilt University in 2021.

Drawing inspiration from the medieval iconography of the court jester, Dylan's work exposes the hypocrisies and uncomfortable truths of modern society. His films dissect technological alienation, performative identity, and the systems of power that shape our lives—delivering pointed social critiques through visceral genre storytelling that refuses to look away.

Dylan's films have screened at festivals across the United States and internationally, garnering recognition for their distinctive voice and uncompromising vision.

When not working on his next film project, Dylan can be found writing code as a software engineer or passionately cheering on his beloved Boston Celtics.

View Dylan's work on IMDb →

Craig Helfer

Craig Helfer's love for cinema began at age seven when he first saw Jurassic Park in a sold-out theater. That transformative experience sparked a lifelong passion that would shape his career as a writer, director, and cinematographer based in New Jersey.

As the creative force behind Iron Horse Cinema, Craig wrote, directed, and produced 20+ short films that accumulated 300,000+ subscribers and 46 million channel views on YouTube. His work spans corporate storytelling and independent filmmaking, with expertise in cinematography, lighting, and post-production using industry-standard tools including DaVinci Resolve, Adobe Premiere Pro, and camera systems from Arri, RED, Blackmagic, Sony, and Canon.

With over a decade of experience in content creation, Craig has worked with high-profile clients including Hilton, Nissan, Goldman Sachs, and Adidas, creating content that has garnered over 120 million views across digital platforms. His unique ability to craft compelling narratives bridges both commercial and artistic mediums.

A graduate of SUNY Polytechnic with a degree in Professional and Technical Communications, Craig began his career as Program Director for the college TV network and creator of the video game review show GITV. When not behind the camera, he enjoys exploring his passion for screenwriting, voice-over work, and of course, spending quality time with his wife and dog.

View Craig's work at craighelfer.com →

George Wang

George Wang is a New York City-based producer with a lifelong passion for storytelling and a proven track record of bringing creative visions to life. He wrote, directed, and produced his first feature film in high school, premiering it to an audience of over 200 people and earning coverage in local press. 

In college, he founded his school's first filmmaking club, growing it from a small circle of friends to over 70 active members producing 40+ short films, earning recognition at national film festivals, and raising more than $20K in funding. 

Beyond the camera, George brings a sharp business mind to his work, drawing on his background in finance and his experience as a nonprofit treasurer overseeing operational and financial decision-making. 

Varun Giridhar

Varun Giridhar is a writer, director and producer based in New York City that makes films that are grounded in universal emotions—often with a sharp twist of dark humor or an inventive genre inflection. Born in India and brought up in America, Varun seeks to bring a global perspective to his creative work that prioritizes the audience’s experience of watching a film.

In college, Varun wrote and directed several short films and a feature film. After graduation, he has written and directed eight short films (the ninth of which is currently in pre-production) and has written four feature specs (the fifth of which is in development.) Varun also writes prose, having written several short stories and a novel as well.

As a filmmaker, Varun seeks to achieve something different with each project. He has directed (and sometimes photographed) narrative short films shot on iPhones, digital cameras, on 16mm, and on 35mm. Varun believes each project is an opportunity to test one’s limits both technically and emotionally, and that a filmmaker should strive for innovation at every chance, from the screenwriting process all the way to the late stages of post-production.

Varun works as a software engineer and is an avid tennis player and fan. When he’s not making films, writing code, or playing or watching tennis, you can probably find him at one of the many repertory movie theaters in New York.

item.title: Contact
item.dc:creator: newworldfoolproductions
item.content:encoded: We'd love to collaborate with you on your next film, music video, or commercial. Reach out below and let's create something extraordinary together:

item.title: Piggy
item.dc:creator: newworldfoolproductions
item.content:encoded:
Director: Dylan Gaines

Writers: Dylan Gaines, Jared Apper

Producers: Dylan Gaines, Craig Helfer, George Wang

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: After bombing yet another open mic, a struggling obese comedian takes drastic measures to obtain the validation he’s always craved.

item.title: Accent
item.dc:creator: newworldfoolproductions
item.content:encoded:
Director: Varun Giridhar

Writer: Varun Giridhar

Producers: Varun Giridhar, George Wang

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: “Accent” is a film about Vaibhav, an international student and computer science TA, who’s in the process of changing his Indian accent to assimilate into American culture. When he overhears a pejorative comment made by his fellow TAs, he decides to confront them, to disastrous consequences.
